figures.caption: Figure 4: Memory–latency Pareto frontier across synthetic distributions and genomics datasets (𝑁 = 100,000 for synthetic, real sizes for genomics). Each trajectory sweeps 𝛼 from 0.5 (right, more memory) to 0.99 (left, less memory). Log-log scale.
Table 1: Synthetic benchmark results (𝑁 = 100,000).Memory in bits/key (bpk), query latency in nanoseconds (ns), and construction time in seconds. Bold indicates best in each column.

Figure 1: Results of sweeping 𝛼 for each distribution across all four filter types. Each panel plots the lower bound (blue dashed), best empirical savings (red dashed), and the upper bound at the theory-guided and empirical-best parameters (light and dark blue solid). The vertical dashed line marks where the lower bound crosses zero.
Figure 3: Results of comparing heuristic and AutoCSF cost models
Table 2: Genomics benchmark results. Memory in bits/key (bpk), query latency in nanoseconds (ns), and construction time in seconds. Bold indicates best in each column.
